жҺҲжҘӯзӣ®зҡ„: This course is more advanced than English Communication A/B/C/D. Students should have already completed Contemporary Topics 1. The objectives of this course are:1. To develop studentsвҖҷ English proficiency through the study of academic topics in the field of sociology.2. To build studentsвҖҷ confidence in using English as a communicative tool.3. To help students improve their listening and note-taking skills through extensive lecture-listening activities. 4. To introduce students to significant vocabulary words relevant to a wide range of academic topics. 5. To prepare students to actively take part in discussions, teaching them the fundamental skills for agreeing and disagreeing with othersвҖҷ opinions.
еҲ°йҒ”зӣ®жЁҷ: 1. Students will develop their academic skills. 2. Students will become more confident and effective communicators.3. Students will strengthen their listening comprehension, note-taking and discussion skills.4. Students will acquire new vocabulary that will enhance their academic achievement.

name: гҖҗ200гҖ‘English Communication Advanced AгҖҖпј“пјҸEnglish Communication Advanced A
жҲҗзёҫи©•дҫЎеӮҷиҖғ:
еӮҷиҖғгҖҖNote
1. Active participation is extremely important and counts as a significant part of studentsвҖҷ evaluation marks.
 2. Attendance is crucial to studentsвҖҷ success in this course. The instructor will carefully mark studentsвҖҷ attendance as present and on time, late, or absent. Specific rules regarding attendance are as follows:
2a) Only students who are present at the time the class begins will receive full attendance marks.
2b) Any student walking into the classroom after the class has begun will be marked late. The instructor will take this into account when giving students a participation grade.
2c) Being 15 to 29 minutes late will equal half an absence (0.5).
2d) Each time students are late 30 minutes or more, they will be marked absent. However, they are still encouraged to attend. 
2e) As a general rule, no more than three absences will be permitted in this course.
